channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: US seizes Iranian container ship Touska in international waters
item.description: US forces boarded and seized the Iranian-flagged container ship Touska, which was reportedly carrying dual-use equipment. Iran condemned the seizure as a ceasefire violation as the truce deadline nears.

item.title: Hungary opposition leader pledges to arrest Netanyahu if elected PM
item.description: Peter Magyar, leader of Hungary opposition, says he will respect ICC obligations by detaining the Israeli leader and reverse Orbans decision to quit the ICC.

item.title: Worlds largest condom maker to raise prices 30% due to Iran war shipping disruption
item.description: Malaysian condom manufacturer Karex announced price hikes of up to 30% as freight costs surge and shipping delays hit supplies due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Xi Jinping offers to work with Africa on Middle East conflict spillover
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping said China is willing to cooperate with African nations to address the economic and humanitarian impacts of the Middle East conflict.

item.title: UK announces measures to delink electricity prices from global gas amid Mideast war
item.description: Britain unveiled reforms aimed at weakening the connection between global gas prices and electricity costs, as Middle East conflict drives energy market volatility.

item.title: US Seizes Iranian Tanker in International Waters as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: The US military boarded and seized a tanker linked to Iran in international waters, enforcing a blockade as time runs out on the ceasefire and last-ditch peace talks remain uncertain.

item.title: Canada Vows to Defend Arctic Alone as Carney Charts New Sovereignty Course
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney pledged Canada will protect the Arctic without outside help, unveiling a military operation to demonstrate sovereignty amid Trump's annexation threats.

item.title: NATO Criticizes Russian and Chinese Nuclear Policies
item.description: NATO urged Russia and China to work with the United States on nuclear arms transparency and stability at an upcoming international conference, criticizing their current nuclear stances.

item.title: Japan Overhauls Defense Export Rules, Opens Global Arms Market
item.description: Japan announced its biggest defense export rule changes in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms sales and enabling exports of warships, missiles, and other weapons.

item.title: Trump China Policy Drifts as Tariffs Stall
item.description: President Trump's tariff strategy toward China has stalled, with trade policy lacking direction as the administration struggles to reset relations.

item.title: EU Seeks US Jet Fuel and SAF to Counter Iran War Strain
item.description: The European Commission announced guidance on airport slots, fuel supplies, and passenger rights as jet fuel shortages loom due to the Iran war.

item.title: Vance to Depart for Iran Peace Talks in Pakistan
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and top US officials are expected to travel to Islamabad for potential Iran peace negotiations as the ceasefire deadline approaches.

item.title: US Navy Minesweepers Head Toward Middle East as Hormuz Clearance Operation Shapes Up
item.description: Avenger-class mine hunters USS Chief and USS Pioneer have been tracked sailing westward from Japan as Trump announces an operation to clear Iranian mines from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Belarus Building Roads and Artillery Positions Near Ukraine Border, Zelensky Warns
item.description: President Zelensky says Russia may try to draw Belarus deeper into the war as new military infrastructure appears along Ukraine's northern frontier.

item.title: US Army Deploys Low-Cost Ukraine-Tested Drones to Counter Iranian Shahed Threat
item.description: Merops interceptor drones, priced at ,000 per unit, offer a cost-effective defense against Iranian Shahed drones costing ,000-,000. The Army has already purchased 13,000 units.

item.title: Pentagon Reaches Out to US Automakers for Weapons Production as Wars Drain Stocks
item.description: Defense officials have contacted GM, Ford, GE Aerospace, and Oshkosh about using factory capacity for military gear as Iran and Ukraine conflicts deplete US munitions.

item.title: Major Natural Gas Discovery Off Indonesia Could Triple Eni Production by 2028
item.description: Eni announced a 141 billion cubic meter gas find off East Kalimantan that could boost Indonesia's energy production and reduce oil imports amid global supply concerns.

item.title: Nigeria Charges Six With Treason Over Alleged Coup Plot
item.description: Nigerian authorities say they foiled a plot to overthrow President Bola Tinubu and charged six people including a retired major general and a serving police inspector with terrorism and treason.

item.title: South Africa Police Chief Charged in $21 Million Corruption Scandal
item.description: National police commissioner Fannie Masemola faces charges over an unlawful contract, marking a major blow to law enforcement leadership in one of Africa's largest economies.

item.title: Trump and Top Republicans Join Marathon Bible Reading Event
item.description: President Donald Trump and conservative Christian supporters are participating in a marathon Bible reading event, highlighting the administration's outreach to evangelical voters.

item.title: EU Court Rules Hungary LGBTQ Law Violates EU Law
item.description: The European Court of Justice found that Hungary's legislation banning LGBTQ content for minors violates EU law, in a landmark ruling with implications for rule-of-law disputes.

item.title: Bloomberg: Can China Escape the Japanification Trap?
item.description: As China's economy slows, analysts debate whether Beijing can avoid the decades-long stagnation that plagued Japan, with implications for global growth.

item.title: Xi Jinping Calls for Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping told Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman that the strategic waterway should remain open, reflecting Beijing's difficult balancing act between Iran and Gulf states during the conflict.

item.title: Japan Issues Megaquake Advisory After 7.7 Magnitude Earthquake
item.description: A powerful earthquake off northern Japan triggered tsunami alerts and a rare advisory warning of slightly elevated megaquake risk. Officials urged preparedness in 182 coastal towns.

item.title: Chad Deploys 1,500 Troops to Haiti for UN Gang Suppression Force
item.description: Chad will send two battalions to join the Kenya-led multinational force combating gang violence in Haiti, where armed groups control most of Port-au-Prince.
